node_backbone: a single {subject}, one isolated 3D game prop, just one object alone, low-poly hard-surface model built from LARGE FLAT POLYGON FACES meeting at CRISP STRAIGHT EDGES, flat shading, every plane clearly visible, no smooth curved surfaces, no rounded chamfers, no bevelled fillets, no photographic detail, simple readable silhouette, muted desaturated colours, matte with no gloss, nothing glowing, no crystals, three-quarter view slightly above the horizon, the whole object complete and uncropped, centered with a generous empty margin on a plain uniform flat light-grey backdrop, no ground, no horizon, no cast shadow, no vignette, no text, neutral stone grey the colour of bare granite, no tan, no beige, no sandstone, no orange or brown tint, medium value that is neither near-white nor near-black
icon_backbone: a single {subject}, one isolated 3D game prop, just one object alone, low-poly, flat shading, faceted geometry with visible polygon faces and crisp hard edges, simple readable silhouette, muted earthy desaturated colours, matte with no gloss, nothing glowing, one soft key light from the upper left with gentle ambient fill so upper facets read clearly brighter than lower ones, three-quarter view about thirty degrees above the horizon, the object wider than tall and filling most of the frame width, mass resting on an implied undrawn ground plane, centered on a plain uniform flat light-grey backdrop, no ground, no horizon, no cast shadow, no vignette, no text, neutral stone grey the colour of bare granite, no tan, no beige, no sandstone, no orange or brown tint, medium value that is neither near-white nor near-black

node_prompts.label: broad squat dome
node_prompts.subject: faceted grey granite boulder studded with darker grey rock knobs
node_prompts.extra: Broad squat dome, twice as wide as tall, knee high. Neutral mid-grey granite body, no tan or beige. Seven blocky slightly darker grey rock knobs of the same granite clustered on the crown with paler grey top facets, two strays low on the flanks.

node_prompts.label: cleaved sheared wedge
node_prompts.subject: cleaved faceted grey granite outcrop with darker grey rock knobs
node_prompts.extra: Angular wedge cleaved by one huge flat sheared face across the front, a few big planes stepping back behind it. Neutral mid-grey granite body, no tan or beige. Banded horizontal seam of slightly darker grey rock knobs of the same granite across the shear face, paler grey top facets. Low broad stance.

node_prompts.label: multi-lobe outcrop
node_prompts.subject: multi-lobed faceted grey granite boulder with darker grey rock knobs
node_prompts.extra: Three fused lobes of unequal size forming one low knuckled mound, wider than tall. Neutral mid-grey granite body, no tan or beige. Small slightly darker grey rock knobs of the same granite scattered in the saddles and around every side, paler grey top facets, two loose lumps at the foot.

node_prompts.label: craggy tilted upthrust
node_prompts.subject: craggy tilted faceted grey granite upthrust studded with darker grey rock knobs
node_prompts.extra: Low leaning deposit, knee high and no taller than it is wide, stepped blocky top and a flared foot. Neutral mid-grey granite body, no tan or beige. Diagonal seam of slightly darker grey rock knobs of the same granite up the leading ridge, paler grey top facets, a small cluster at the base.

icon_prompts.subject: chunky faceted grey stone block
icon_prompts.extra: One fist-sized angular stone. Neutral mid-grey granite, no tan or beige. Chunky readable planes, two or three tonal steps per face, mild painted grain.

icon_prompts.label: tight four-piece cluster
icon_prompts.subject: tight cluster of four chunky faceted grey stone cobbles
icon_prompts.extra: Four angular cobbles of varied size nested together, largest in front. Neutral mid-grey granite, no tan or beige. Chunky readable planes, two or three tonal steps per face, mild painted grain.

icon_prompts.label: low broad heap

icon_prompts.extra: Loose low pile, a few pieces stacked on the crown and two spilled at the foot. Neutral mid-grey granite, no tan or beige. Chunky readable planes, two or three tonal steps per face.

notes:
Two backbones now carry every generic instruction, each with one {subject} token. The node backbone front-loads the image-to-3D requirement (large flat polygon faces, crisp straight edges, and an explicit ban on smooth curves, rounded chamfers, bevelled fillets and photographic detail) plus the single-complete-uncropped-object framing. The icon backbone hard-locks the three axes the audit found broken: one camera (three-quarter, thirty degrees above horizon), one key direction (soft key from upper left with ambient fill, upper facets brighter), and one subject scale (wider than tall, filling most of the frame width, resting on an implied undrawn ground plane). Both ban cast shadow, vignette, ground, horizon and text, so sulfur's shadow-broken centering cannot recur.

Every extra was rewritten to say only what the backbone does not: silhouette, chunk placement, colour, surface character, proportion. All references to view angle, margin, backdrop, faceting-in-general, matte, gloss and shadow were deleted from the extras. Every extra is under 45 words, contains no em dashes, and the words icon and inventory icon appear nowhere inside a prompt.

Cross-type parallelism is now exact. All five types use the same four node labels (broad squat dome, cleaved sheared wedge, multi-lobe outcrop, craggy tilted upthrust) and the same four icon labels (single hero lump, tight four-piece cluster, low broad heap, cleaved open piece). Within each node theme the four grey types share a literally identical sentence skeleton, so only the mineral clause differs: dome = seven chunks crowned plus two flank strays, wedge = banded horizontal seam across the shear face, outcrop = studs in the saddles and around every side plus two foot lumps, upthrust = diagonal seam up the leading ridge plus a base cluster. That also preserves the source rationales' intent (crown cluster, seam, 360 degree scatter, vertical read) while making the picker grid compare like with like.

Shared-grey-rock-body rule enforced verbatim: stone, iron, coal and sulfur all read "Bright warm-grey rock body" in the same sentence position, and identity lives only in the chunk clause with its chunk plus brighter-top-facet pair, matching the COLOR_0 split. Coal's chunks always carry an explicit lighter graphite-grey top facet so near-black lumps do not collapse into a silhouette hole once the three cel bands quantise. Sulfur is repeatedly pinned to dusty mustard rather than gold to kill the measured 0.66 to 0.885 saturation blowout. Meteorite keeps its brief-mandated exception (sooty charcoal-black burnt slag body, pale silver-grey iron-nickel nuggets) and is described as squatter and stouter than the grey family in every variant, including the upthrust, which is worded as low and stout rather than tall.

Icon family: proportion, count grammar and surface language are now uniform. Variants 2 to 4 are the pile grammar that coal and stone already use for raw bulk materials, variant 1 is the deliberate single-object counter-option, and every extra specifies chunky readable planes with two or three tonal steps per face so the smooth iron_ore and sulfur_ore treatment cannot diverge from the crunchy stone and coal treatment. Meteorite icons explicitly say no cracks or veining, removing the photographic cracked-marble and blown-white failure.

Magic and glow language is fully absent: no emissive, no crystals, no shards of light, no runes. Both backbones state nothing glowing, and the node backbone additionally states no crystals.
